skill.name: flow-brainstorming
skill.description: Guides requirement brainstorming before implementation by capturing original intent, exploring options, and recording decisions in BRAINSTORM.md.
skill.summary: Clarifies vague feature ideas before implementation starts.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts are also false positives caused by Chinese prose and Markdown content. The remaining concern is low-risk filesystem guidance because the skill asks the assistant to create and read planning documents inside the project.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences and formatted examples. No executable command invocation, shell expansion, or Ruby backtick execution was found in the inspected skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain Markdown fences and documentation examples only. There is no code path that executes these fenced blocks as commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashing algorithms, encryption calls, or password handling. They are prose and a section heading in the skill documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible documentation text, not executable code. No weak algorithm name or cryptographic operation appears at those locations.

low_findings.title: Static Entropy Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The entropy alerts appear to be caused by multilingual Markdown content, especially Chinese text in metadata and instructions. No encoded payload, compressed binary data, or obfuscated script was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is readable Markdown and the high-entropy line is Chinese prose. This supports a benign multilingual-content explanation.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Planning Document Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to create, read, and update BRAINSTORM.md and related project planning files. This is aligned with the skill purpose but should remain visible as a filesystem modification risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines explicitly reference writing, reading, and updating planning documents. The behavior is legitimate for the workflow but involves local project files.

content.value_statement: Vague requests often lead to rework and mismatched implementation choices. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused questions, option comparison, and documented decisions.
actual_capabilities: Turns vague feature ideas into a structured requirement discussion.
Prompts the assistant to ask one focused question at a time.
Compares two or three implementation approaches with tradeoffs.
Records original intent, constraints, success criteria, and decisions.
Adds alignment checks before later workflow phases continue.
limitations: It does not implement code or run tests by itself.
It depends on user answers to resolve ambiguous requirements.
It assumes the project follows the CC-DevFlow requirement structure.
It may need adaptation for teams that use a different planning format.

use_cases.title: Define a New Feature
use_cases.description: Use the skill to turn a rough product idea into a documented scope, success criteria, and recommended approach.

use_cases.title: Align Before Refactoring
use_cases.description: Use the skill to capture the reason for a refactor, compare options, and avoid unnecessary technical changes.

use_cases.title: Preserve Decision Context
use_cases.description: Use the skill to document why a solution was selected so later implementation phases can check for drift.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Brainstorm
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-brainstorming for this request. Ask me one question at a time and help define the core problem, constraints, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-brainstorming to propose two or three approaches for this requirement. Explain tradeoffs, recommend one option, and wait for my decision.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a BRAINSTORM.md Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-brainstorming to summarize our confirmed requirement into a BRAINSTORM.md draft with original intent, success criteria, constraints, options, and final decision.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Alignment Before Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-brainstorming to check whether the current implementation plan still matches the original intent and selected approach. Identify any drift before work continues.

output_examples.input: I want to add notifications, but I am not sure what kind.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks a single multiple-choice question about the main notification goal, then narrows scope through constraints and success criteria.

output_examples.input: We need to improve search performance.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares indexing, caching, and query optimization, explains tradeoffs, and recommends the option that best fits the stated constraints.

output_examples.input: Before implementation, confirm that the plan still matches the original requirement.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the recorded intent, selected approach, and constraints, then flags any mismatch for user confirmation.

best_practices: Provide the original user request before asking the skill to refine it.
Answer each clarification question before moving to solution design.
Review the final decision record before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to skip user confirmation on important tradeoffs.
Do not treat the first proposed option as final without comparing alternatives.
Do not continue later workflow phases when the plan no longer matches the recorded intent.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on requirement discovery, option comparison, and planning documentation before implementation.

faq.question: Can it work outside CC-DevFlow?
faq.answer: Yes, but the document paths and workflow gates may need adjustment for another process.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: This keeps requirement discovery focused and reduces the chance that the user misses an important assumption.

faq.question: What file does it expect to produce?
faq.answer: It expects a BRAINSTORM.md document that records intent, constraints, options, and the final decision.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation guidance, with only low-risk project file instructions.

faq.question: Does it make network requests or execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The command-related static alerts are Markdown formatting false positives.
